title: Treat Numbness in Hands with These 7 Powerful Herbal Decoctions

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bilobalide is good for numbness in hands because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and improve blood circulation to the affected area, thereby relieving numbness and pain.

Quercetin in Achillea Millefolium is good for numbness in hands because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and improve blood flow to the hands, thereby relieving numbness and tingling sensations.
Kaempferol is good for numbness in hands because it helps to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Ginkgolide B is good for numbness in hands because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and improve blood flow to the hands, which can alleviate numbness and tingling sensations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions can help with numbness in hands due to its ability to improve blood flow to the affected area. This is because ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are natural compounds that help to widen blood vessels and increase circulation. As a result, more oxygen and nutrients are delivered to the hands, which can help to reduce numbness and tingling. Additionally, ginkgo biloba may also help to reduce inflammation in the nerves, which can contribute to numbness and pain. By improving circulation and reducing inflammation, ginkgo biloba decoctions may provide relief from numbness in the hands. The improved blood flow and reduced inflammation can also help to speed up the healing process of damaged nerves.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal decoctions that help with numbness in hands, start by gathering fresh or dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. You can either purchase them from a health food store or grow them yourself in a garden. If using fresh herbs, rinse them thoroughly with water and pat them dry with a clean cloth. Then, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the herbs with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan. Reduce heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-10 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon juice to taste if desired. Let the decoction cool down and drink it 2-3 times a day. Some people also like to soak their hands in the cooled decoction for 10-15 minutes to help relieve numbness. It's essential to note that Achillea millefolium may interact with certain medications, so consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help with numbness in hands. The roots of the ginger plant contain a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When ginger is made into a decoction - a liquid extract by boiling the roots in water - it can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands and fingers. This can be especially helpful for people who experience numbness due to conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome or Raynaud's disease. Ginger decoctions can also improve blood flow and circulation, which is essential for delivering oxygen and nutrients to the hands and fingers, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties that can help protect the hands and fingers from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing numbness and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal decoction that helps with numbness in hands, start by gathering dried or fresh yarrow flowers. If using fresh flowers, dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for a few days. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh yarrow flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup. Discard the solids and let the decoction cool slightly. To use, soak your hands in the warm decoction for 10-15 minutes, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help reduce numbness and improve circulation. You can also add 1-2 tablespoons of the decoction to your bath water for an overall relaxing and circulation-boosting experience.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, is a popular herb used to treat various health issues, including numbness in hands. The main reason rosemary is beneficial for numbness is because of its anti-inflammatory properties. When inflammation occurs in the nerves, it can cause numbness, tingling, and pain in the hands. Rosemary decoctions contain compounds like camphor and borneol, which help reduce inflammation and ease pain. Additionally, rosemary contains antioxidants that protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals, which can also contribute to numbness. Drinking rosemary decoctions may help increase blood flow to the hands, promoting healing and reducing numbness. Some people also find that rosemary's stimulating properties help improve circulation and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to numbness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal decoction for numbness in hands, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves, flowers, and stems of the plant. Dry the plant material in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying it, and then grind it into a fine powder. Combine one teaspoon of the powder with one cup of boiling water in a saucepan, and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes. Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the solids. Let the decoction cool down, then drink it three times a day. Alternatively, you can make a stronger decoction by steeping the plant material in a cup of boiling water for 15-20 minutes, then straining and cooling it before drinking. As you use the decoction, observe the effects on your numbness in hands and adjust the dosage accordingly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum decoction, also known as black pepper extract, may help numbness in hands due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The capsaicin in black pepper can block pain receptors in the body, reducing numbness and pain caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome. When consumed as a decoction, the capsaicin is absorbed into the bloodstream, where it can target areas affected by numbness. This may help improve blood flow and reduce swelling in the affected area, providing relief from numbness and discomfort. Additionally, piper nigrum decoction has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation. By using piper nigrum decoction, some people may find that their numbness in hands improves, allowing them to perform daily activities with more ease and comfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an herbal decoction using Achillea millefolium to help with numbness in hands, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried yarrow flowers and leaves. Rinse the herbs under cold running water, then gently pat them dry with a paper towel. Place the herbs in a small saucepan and add 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their active compounds. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the decoction steep for another 5-10 minutes. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and transfer the decoction to a clean glass bottle. To use, drink 1/4 to 1/2 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals or before bedtime, as the herbs may cause drowsiness. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Achillea millefolium, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking any medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum decoctions have been found to be beneficial for numbness in hands due to their warming and stimulating properties. The active compound capsaicin in Capsicum annuum is responsible for this effect. When capsaicin comes into contact with nerve endings in the skin, it releases a chemical called substance P, which sends a signal to the brain. This signal is perceived as heat or warmth, and it increases blood flow to the affected area, helping to relieve numbness and stiffness. As a result, the numbness in hands is reduced, and circulation is improved, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach the tissues more efficiently. This can lead to a reduction in symptoms associated with numbness, such as tingling and weakness. Regular use of Capsicum annuum decoctions may also help to increase nerve sensitivity and promote healing in the affected area.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal decoction that helps with numbness in hands, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried yarrow flowers, leaves, and stems. Rinse them gently with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Combine the yarrow parts with one quart of boiling water in a saucepan, then reduce heat to a simmer. Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together. Next, strain the decoction into a clean container, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature. Once cooled, you can drink the decoction as is, or add a sweetener like honey or maple syrup if desired. For best results, drink 1-2 cups of the yarrow decoction daily, ideally after meals or at bedtime, to help alleviate numbness in your hands.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa decoction, also known as turmeric root, is a traditional remedy that has been used to treat numbness in hands. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. This leads to improved blood flow to the hands, relieving numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, curcumin has antioxidant properties that protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to numbness. Turmeric also has a warming effect, which can help to increase circulation and reduce stiffness in the hands and fingers. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, turmeric decoction can help to alleviate numbness in hands and restore sensation and mobility.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal decoctions for numbness in hands, start by gathering a handful of dried or fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. If using fresh, rinse them under cold water and pat dry with a paper towel. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the dried leaves and flowers in a small saucepan with 1 cup of boiling water. Alternatively, use 2-3 tablespoons of fresh leaves and flowers. Cover the saucepan and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties. After the steeping time is up, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids. Let the decoction cool down to a comfortable temperature before applying it to the affected area using a cloth or a compress. It is recommended to use this decoction 2-3 times a day, or as needed, to help alleviate numbness in the hands.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, has been used for centuries to help relieve numbness in hands. The main reason for its effectiveness is its ability to improve blood circulation. When you make a decoction from the leaves and flowers of the yarrow plant, it releases compounds that help to expand blood vessels and promote the flow of blood to the hands. This increased blood flow brings more oxygen and nutrients to the nerves, helping to relieve numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, yarrow decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with numbness in the hands. By using Achillea millefolium decoctions regularly, you can help to improve your circulation and alleviate numbness in your hands. The decoction can be consumed as a tea, and it is recommended to drink it 2-3 times a day to see the best results.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal decoction that helps with numbness in hands, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves, flowers, and stems of the plant. If using fresh parts, chop them finely and dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying until completely dry. Fill a saucepan with 2 cups of water and add 1-2 tablespoons of dried Achillea millefolium, depending on your preference for strength. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-7 minutes. Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool slightly before using it as a topical treatment. Soak your hands in the warm decoction for 10-15 minutes, or apply it directly to the affected area with a cotton pad. Repeat the process 2-3 times a day for best results.

intro_desc: If you're experiencing numbness in your hands, it's essential to address the issue. Numbness can be caused by poor circulation, nerve damage, or other underlying health problems. If left untreated, it can lead to reduced mobility, decreased dexterity, and increased risk of injury. To help alleviate numbness in your hands, consider using herbal decoctions that promote circulation and nerve health.  Ginkgo biloba is a popular herb that can help improve blood flow to your hands, reducing numbness and tingling. Its active compounds, flavonoids and terpenoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help repair damaged blood vessels and improve circulation. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, is another herb that can aid in circulation and reduce inflammation. Its warming properties can help increase blood flow to your hands, reducing numbness and discomfort.  To make a herbal decoction for numbness in your hands, combine Ginkgo biloba and Zingiber officinale in hot water. Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain and drink. You can also add other herbs like peppermint or chamomile to enhance the flavor and benefits. Drinking herbal decoctions like this can help improve your overall circulation and reduce numbness in your hands. By addressing this issue, you can improve your daily life by being able to perform tasks with more ease and precision, such as typing, cooking, or even simple tasks like buttoning your shirt. This can also help you regain confidence and independence, allowing you to live a more active and fulfilling life.
supplementary_best: To remedy numbness in hands, a combination of Rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary) and Piper nigrum (black pepper) decoctions is recommended. Rosmarinus officinalis contains compounds that improve blood circulation, which helps to reduce numbness and tingling sensations in hands. Piper nigrum, on the other hand, contains piperine, a bioactive compound that enhances the absorption of other herbs, making their effects more potent. The combination of rosemary and black pepper decoctions is effective because rosemary's circulation-improving properties are amplified by black pepper's absorption-enhancing properties. This synergy ensures that the body receives the benefits of rosemary's circulation-improving compounds, leading to faster relief from numbness in hands. The recommended dosage is to drink a decoction of 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary and 1/2 teaspoon of dried black pepper in 1 cup of boiling water, 2-3 times a day.
supplementary_avoid: If you have numbness in your hands, there are certain herbal decoctions you should avoid. One of these is Angelica root, as it can slow down blood flow and make numbness worse. Another is Black cohosh, which can cause numbness and tingling in some people due to its effect on nerve cells. Also, avoid Goldenseal, as it contains a chemical that can increase blood pressure and worsen numbness. Feverfew is another herb to steer clear of, as it can cause numbness, tingling, and pain in some individuals. And, finally, avoid Valerian root, as it can cause numbness, drowsiness, and dizziness in some people, which may worsen existing numbness in hands.
supplementary_other_remedies: Besides herbal decoctions, another natural remedy that can help with numbness in hands is Epsom salt baths. When you soak your hands in warm water with Epsom salt, the magnesium in it is absorbed through your skin. Magnesium is a mineral that helps relax muscles and improve blood flow, which can reduce numbness and tingling sensations. The warm water also increases blood circulation, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach your hands. This can help repair damaged nerve endings and reduce numbness.  Another natural remedy is essential oil massage. Certain essential oils like peppermint, eucalyptus, and rosemary have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease numbness. When you massage these oils into your skin, they can stimulate blood flow and relax tense muscles, which can help numbness in your hands. This is because the oils can penetrate deep into your skin and reach the affected areas, providing relief from numbness and tingling sensations.  Aromatherapy also plays a role in relieving numbness in hands. Inhaling certain essential oils can help calm your nerves and reduce stress, which can contribute to numbness. When you breathe in the scent of essential oils, they can stimulate your brain's limbic system, which is responsible for emotional responses and relaxation. This can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to numbness in your hands.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience numbness in their hands may also have other health issues. One common condition is diabetes. Medicinal herbs like ginseng and berberine can help lower blood sugar levels and improve circulation, which can alleviate numbness.  Another possible issue is hypothyroidism, a condition where the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough hormones. This can cause numbness, tingling, and pain in the hands and feet. Herbs like ashwagandha and Bladderwrack can help stimulate the thyroid gland and improve hormone production.  Some people may experience carpal tunnel syndrome, which is caused by compression of the median nerve in the wrist. Ginger and turmeric can help reduce inflammation and relieve pressure on the nerve, providing relief from numbness.  Additionally, people with Raynaud's disease may experience numbness in their hands and fingers due to poor blood flow. Herbs like cayenne pepper and ginkgo biloba can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the frequency and severity of numbness episodes.
